name: refund-window
input: I paid for my monthly plan 20 days ago and I'm not using it. Can I get a refund?
expected_output: No. Acme's refund window is 14 days from the charge, so a 20-day-old payment is outside it. Offer to cancel so they aren't billed again next cycle (access continues until the period ends).

name: annual-refund
input: I switched to the annual plan 10 days ago but need to cancel. Do I get anything back?
expected_output: Yes. Annual plans get a prorated refund if cancelled within 30 days of purchase, and 10 days qualifies. After 30 days, annual plans are non-refundable.

name: downgrade-midcycle
input: I just downgraded from Pro to Basic. Where's my partial refund for the rest of the month?
expected_output: Downgrades take effect at the next billing cycle and are not refunded mid-cycle. They keep Pro features until the current period ends, then move to Basic with no proration.

name: upgrade-proration
input: If I upgrade from Basic to Pro halfway through the month, do I pay anything now?
expected_output: Yes. Upgrades apply immediately and are prorated, so they're charged the Pro/Basic difference for the remainder of the current cycle right away.

name: trial-terms
input: How long is the free trial, and do I need to enter a credit card?
expected_output: The free trial is 14 days and a credit card is required. It auto-converts to a paid subscription at the end of the trial unless cancelled before it ends.

name: coupon-stacking
input: Can I apply a promo coupon on top of the annual discount?
expected_output: No. Only one discount applies per subscription, and coupons do not stack with the annual discount. The customer keeps whichever discount is larger.

name: seat-removal
input: We removed 2 seats this week. Will you refund those seats to my card?
expected_output: Removed seats are not refunded to the card; their value is credited to the next invoice. Added seats, by contrast, are prorated and charged immediately.

name: pause-subscription
input: Things are slow this quarter. Can I pause my subscription instead of cancelling?
expected_output: Yes. Monthly plans can be paused for up to 3 months. Billing is suspended while paused and resumes automatically when the pause ends.

name: cancel-access
input: If I cancel today, do I lose access right away?
expected_output: No. Access continues until the end of the current paid period; cancellation only stops future renewals. There's no partial refund for the unused time (except an annual plan within its 30-day window).

name: frustrated-pricing
input: Honestly your product feels too expensive for what we use. Not sure it's worth it.
expected_output: Respond with empathy, acknowledge the cost concern, and offer concrete options: the annual plan discount, downgrading to a cheaper tier, or removing unused seats (credited to the next invoice). Avoid being defensive.
